title: Grilling Wizard Demo — Feature Flag Strategy
summary: Demonstrates the Maestro grilling wizard UI: structured question prompts, option selection, progress tracking, and the full answered/unanswered message thread. Open /grill/{id} to see the wizard in action.

items.text: This plan file is a self-contained demo of the grilling wizard. Its messages array contains agent prompts with structured question_key, options, and answered flags.
The wizard page at /grill/{id} scans messages newest-first for the latest unanswered prompt and renders it as an interactive question card.
Progress badge (Q 2/5) appears when total_questions is set on the prompt.
Use a SaaS feature flag service (LaunchDarkly)
items.options: build in-house with Unleash; SaaS with LaunchDarkly; open-source Flagsmith
items.rationale: LaunchDarkly wins on SDK maturity, multi-language support, and audit trail. The team is small and a managed service avoids operational overhead.

items.text: Target full rollout over 3 phases across 6 weeks
items.options: big-bang rollout (1 week); phased rollout (6 weeks); canary-only indefinitely
items.rationale: Phased rollout balances velocity with safety. Internal dogfood → 10% production → full rollout gives each phase time to surface issues.

messages.text: What is the primary goal for introducing feature flags?
Should we build a feature flag system in-house or use a SaaS provider?
What rollout strategy should we adopt?

options: Big-bang rollout (1 week, all environments at once)
